translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: After the death of Saul, when David had returned from the slaughter of the Amalekites, and David had stayed two days in Ziklag;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: on the third day, behold,  a man came out of the camp from Saul, with his clothes torn, and earth on his head. When he came to David, he fell to the earth, and showed respect.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: David said to him, “Where do you come from?” He said to him, “I have escaped out of the camp of Israel.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: David said to him, “How did it go? Please tell me.” He answered, “The people have fled from the battle, and many of the people also have fallen and are dead. Saul and Jonathan his son are dead also.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: David said to the young man who told him, “How do you know that Saul and Jonathan his son are dead?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The young man who told him said, “As I happened by chance on Mount Gilboa, behold, Saul was leaning on his spear; and behold, the chariots and the horsemen followed close behind him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When he looked behind him, he saw me, and called to me. I answered, ‘Here I am.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to me, ‘Who are you?’ I answered him, ‘I am an Amalekite.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to me, ‘Please stand beside me, and kill me; for anguish has taken hold of me, because my life lingers in me.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So I stood beside him and killed him, because I was sure that he could not live after that he had fallen. I took the crown that was on his head and the bracelet that was on his arm, and have brought them here to my lord.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then David took hold on his clothes, and tore them; and all the men who were with him did likewise.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They mourned, wept, and fasted until evening, for Saul, and for Jonathan his son, and for the people of Yahweh,  and for the house of Israel; because they had fallen by the sword.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: David said to the young man who told him, “Where are you from?” He answered, “I am the son of a foreigner, an Amalekite.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: David said to him, “Why were you not afraid to stretch out your hand to destroy Yahweh’s anointed?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: David called one of the young men, and said, “Go near, and cut him down!” He struck him so that he died.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: David said to him, “Your blood be on your head; for your mouth has testified against you, saying, ‘I have slain Yahweh’s anointed.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: David lamented with this lamentation over Saul and over Jonathan his son

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: (and he commanded them to teach the children of Judah the song of the bow; behold, it is written in the book of Jashar):

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Your glory, Israel, was slain on your high places! How the mighty have fallen!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t tell it in Gath. Don’t publish it in the streets of Ashkelon, lest the daughters of the Philistines rejoice, lest the daughters of the uncircumcised triumph.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You mountains of Gilboa, let there be no dew or rain on you, and no fields of offerings; For there the shield of the mighty was defiled and cast away, The shield of Saul was not anointed with oil.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: From the blood of the slain, from the fat of the mighty, Jonathan’s bow didn’t turn back. Saul’s sword didn’t return empty.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Saul and Jonathan were lovely and pleasant in their lives. In their death, they were not divided. They were swifter than eagles. They were stronger than lions.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You daughters of Israel, weep over Saul, who clothed you delicately in scarlet, who put ornaments of gold on your clothing.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: How the mighty have fallen in the middle of the battle! Jonathan was slain on your high places.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I am distressed for you, my brother Jonathan. You have been very pleasant to me. Your love to me was wonderful, passing the love of women.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: How the mighty have fallen, and the weapons of war have perished!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: After this, David inquired of Yahweh, saying, “Shall I go up into any of the cities of Judah?” Yahweh said to him, “Go up.” David said, “Where shall I go up?” He said, “To Hebron.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So David went up there with his two wives, Ahinoam the Jezreelitess, and Abigail the wife of Nabal the Carmelite.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: David brought up his men who were with him, every man with his household. They lived in the cities of Hebron.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The men of Judah came, and there they anointed David king over the house of Judah. They told David, “The men of Jabesh Gilead were those who buried Saul.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: David sent messengers to the men of Jabesh Gilead, and said to them, “Blessed are you by Yahweh, that you have shown this kindness to your lord, even to Saul, and have buried him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now may Yahweh show loving kindness and truth to you. I also will reward you for this kindness, because you have done this thing.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now therefore let your hands be strong, and be valiant; for Saul your lord is dead, and also the house of Judah have anointed me king over them.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now Abner the son of Ner, captain of Saul’s army, had taken Ishbosheth the son of Saul, and brought him over to Mahanaim;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and he made him king over Gilead, and over the Ashurites, and over Jezreel, and over Ephraim, and over Benjamin, and over all Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Ishbosheth, Saul’s son, was forty years old when he began to reign over Israel, and he reigned two years. But the house of Judah followed David.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The time that David was king in Hebron over the house of Judah was seven years and six months.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Abner the son of Ner, and the servants of Ishbosheth the son of Saul, went out from Mahanaim to Gibeon.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Joab the son of Zeruiah and David’s servants went out, and met them by the pool of Gibeon; and they sat down, the one on the one side of the pool, and the other on the other side of the pool.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Abner said to Joab, “Please let the young men arise and play before us!” Joab said, “Let them arise!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then they arose and went over by number: twelve for Benjamin and for Ishbosheth the son of Saul, and twelve of David’s servants.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They each caught his opponent by the head, and thrust his sword in his fellow’s side; so they fell down together: therefore that place in Gibeon was called Helkath Hazzurim.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The battle was very severe that day; and Abner was beaten, and the men of Israel, before David’s servants.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The three sons of Zeruiah were there, Joab, and Abishai, and Asahel: and Asahel was as light of foot as a wild gazelle.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Asahel pursued Abner; and in going he didn’t turn to the right hand or to the left from following Abner.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Abner looked behind him, and said, “Is that you, Asahel?” He answered, “It is.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Abner said to him, “Turn aside to your right hand or to your left, and grab one of the young men, and take his armor.” But Asahel would not turn aside from following him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Abner said again to Asahel, “Turn aside from following me. Why should I strike you to the ground? How then could I look Joab your brother in the face?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: However he refused to turn aside. Therefore Abner with the back end of the spear struck him in the body, so that the spear came out behind him; and he fell down there, and died in the same place. As many as came to the place where Asahel fell down and died stood still.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But Joab and Abishai pursued Abner. The sun went down when they had come to the hill of Ammah, that lies before Giah by the way of the wilderness of Gibeon.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The children of Benjamin gathered themselves together after Abner, and became one band, and stood on the top of a hill.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Abner called to Joab, and said, “Shall the sword devour forever? Don’t you know that it will be bitterness in the latter end? How long will it be then, before you ask the people to return from following their brothers?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Joab said, “As God  lives, if you had not spoken, surely then in the morning the people would have gone away, and not each followed his brother.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So Joab blew the trumpet; and all the people stood still, and pursued Israel no more, and they fought no more.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Abner and his men went all that night through the Arabah; and they passed over the Jordan, and went through all Bithron, and came to Mahanaim.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Joab returned from following Abner; and when he had gathered all the people together, nineteen men of David’s and Asahel were missing.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But David’s servants had struck Benjamin and of Abner’s men so that three hundred sixty men died.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They took up Asahel, and buried him in the tomb of his father, which was in Bethlehem. Joab and his men went all night, and the day broke on them at Hebron.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now there was long war between Saul’s house and David’s house. David grew stronger and stronger, but Saul’s house grew weaker and weaker.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Sons were born to David in Hebron. His firstborn was Amnon, of Ahinoam the Jezreelitess;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and his second, Chileab, of Abigail the wife of Nabal the Carmelite; and the third, Absalom the son of Maacah the daughter of Talmai king of Geshur;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and the fourth, Adonijah the son of Haggith; and the fifth, Shephatiah the son of Abital;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and the sixth, Ithream, of Eglah, David’s wife. These were born to David in Hebron.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: While there was war between Saul’s house and David’s house, Abner made himself strong in Saul’s house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now Saul had a concubine, whose name was Rizpah, the daughter of Aiah; and Ishbosheth said to Abner, “Why have you gone in to my father’s concubine?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Abner was very angry about Ishbosheth’s words, and said, “Am I a dog’s head that belongs to Judah? Today I show kindness to Saul’s house your father, to his brothers, and to his friends, and have not delivered you into the hand of David; and yet you charge me today with a fault concerning this woman!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: God do so to Abner, and more also, if, as Yahweh has sworn to David, I don’t do even so to him;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: to transfer the kingdom from Saul’s house, and to set up David’s throne over Israel and over Judah, from Dan even to Beersheba.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He could not answer Abner another word, because he was afraid of him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Abner sent messengers to David on his behalf, saying, “Whose is the land?” and saying, “Make your alliance with me, and behold, my hand will be with you, to bring all Israel around to you.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, “Good. I will make a treaty with you, but one thing I require of you. That is, you will not see my face unless you first bring Michal, Saul’s daughter, when you come to see my face.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: David sent messengers to Ishbosheth, Saul’s son, saying, “Deliver me my wife Michal, whom I was given to marry for one hundred foreskins of the Philistines.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Ishbosheth sent and took her from her husband, even from Paltiel the son of Laish.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Her husband went with her, weeping as he went, and followed her to Bahurim. Then Abner said to him, “Go! Return!” and he returned.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Abner had communication with the elders of Israel, saying, “In times past, you sought for David to be king over you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now then do it; for Yahweh has spoken of David, saying, ‘By the hand of my servant David, I will save my people Israel out of the hand of the Philistines, and out of the hand of all their enemies.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Abner also spoke in the ears of Benjamin: and Abner went also to speak in the ears of David in Hebron all that seemed good to Israel, and to the whole house of Benjamin.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So Abner came to David to Hebron, and twenty men with him. David made Abner and the men who were with him a feast.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Abner said to David, “I will arise and go, and will gather all Israel to my lord the king, that they may make a covenant with you, and that you may reign over all that your soul desires.” David sent Abner away; and he went in peace.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, David’s servants and Joab came from a raid, and brought in a great plunder with them; but Abner was not with David in Hebron; for he had sent him away, and he had gone in peace.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Joab and all the army who was with him had come, they told Joab, “Abner the son of Ner came to the king, and he has sent him away, and he has gone in peace.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Joab came to the king, and said, “What have you done? Behold, Abner came to you. Why is it that you have sent him away, and he is already gone?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You know Abner the son of Ner. He came to deceive you, and to know your going out and your coming in, and to know all that you do.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Joab had come out from David, he sent messengers after Abner, and they brought him back from the well of Sirah; but David didn’t know it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Abner was returned to Hebron, Joab took him aside into the middle of the gate to speak with him quietly, and struck him there in the body, so that he died, for the blood of Asahel his brother.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Afterward, when David heard it, he said, “I and my kingdom are guiltless before Yahweh forever of the blood of Abner the son of Ner.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let it fall on the head of Joab, and on all his father’s house. Let there not fail from the house of Joab one who has an issue, or who is a leper, or who leans on a staff, or who falls by the sword, or who lacks bread.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So Joab and Abishai his brother killed Abner, because he had killed their brother Asahel at Gibeon in the battle.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: David said to Joab, and to all the people who were with him, “Tear your clothes, and clothe yourselves with sackcloth, and mourn in front of Abner.” King David followed the bier.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They buried Abner in Hebron; and the king lifted up his voice, and wept at Abner’s grave; and all the people wept.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The king lamented for Abner, and said, “Should Abner die as a fool dies?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Your hands weren’t bound, and your feet weren’t put into fetters. As a man falls before the children of iniquity, so you fell.” All the people wept again over him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All the people came to urge David to eat bread while it was yet day; but David swore, saying, “God do so to me, and more also, if I taste bread, or anything else, until the sun goes down.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All the people took notice of it, and it pleased them; as whatever the king did pleased all the people.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So all the people and all Israel understood that day that it was not of the king to kill Abner the son of Ner.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The king said to his servants, “Don’t you know that there a prince and a great man has fallen today in Israel?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I am weak today, though anointed king. These men, the sons of Zeruiah are too hard for me. May Yahweh reward the evildoer according to his wickedness.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Saul’s son heard that Abner was dead in Hebron, his hands became feeble, and all the Israelites were troubled.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Saul’s son had two men who were captains of raiding bands. The name of one was Baanah, and the name of the other Rechab, the sons of Rimmon the Beerothite, of the children of Benjamin (for Beeroth also is considered a part of Benjamin:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and the Beerothites fled to Gittaim, and have lived as foreigners there until today).

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now Jonathan, Saul’s son, had a son who was lame in his feet. He was five years old when the news came about Saul and Jonathan out of Jezreel; and his nurse picked him up and fled. As she hurried to flee, he fell and became lame. His name was Mephibosheth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The sons of Rimmon the Beerothite, Rechab and Baanah, went and came at about the heat of the day to the house of Ishbosheth, as he took his rest at noon.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They came there into the middle of the house, as though they would have fetched wheat; and they struck him in the body: and Rechab and Baanah his brother escaped.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now when they came into the house, as he lay on his bed in his bedroom, they struck him, killed him, beheaded him, and took his head, and went by the way of the Arabah all night.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They brought the head of Ishbosheth to David to Hebron, and said to the king, “Behold, the head of Ishbosheth, the son of Saul, your enemy, who sought your life! Yahweh has avenged my lord the king today of Saul, and of his offspring. ”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: David answered Rechab and Baanah his brother, the sons of Rimmon the Beerothite, and said to them, “As Yahweh lives, who has redeemed my soul out of all adversity,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: when someone told me, ‘Behold, Saul is dead,’ thinking that he brought good news, I seized him and killed him in Ziklag, which was the reward I gave him for his news.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: How much more, when wicked men have slain a righteous person in his own house on his bed, should I not now require his blood from your hand, and rid the earth of you?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: David commanded his young men, and they killed them, cut off their hands and their feet, and hanged them up beside the pool in Hebron. But they took the head of Ishbosheth, and buried it in Abner’s grave in Hebron.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then all the tribes of Israel came to David at Hebron, and spoke, saying, “Behold, we are your bone and your flesh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In times past, when Saul was king over us, it was you who led Israel out and in. Yahweh said to you, ‘You will be shepherd of my people Israel, and you will be prince over Israel.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So all the elders of Israel came to the king to Hebron, and king David made a covenant with them in Hebron before Yahweh; and they anointed David king over Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: David was thirty years old when he began to reign, and he reigned forty years.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In Hebron he reigned over Judah seven years and six months; and in Jerusalem he reigned thirty-three years over all Israel and Judah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The king and his men went to Jerusalem against the Jebusites, the inhabitants of the land, who spoke to David, saying, “The blind and the lame will keep you out of here”; thinking, “David can’t come in here.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Nevertheless David took the stronghold of Zion. This is David’s city.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: David said on that day, “Whoever strikes the Jebusites, let him go up to the watercourse and strike the lame and the blind, who are hated by David’s soul.” Therefore they say, “The blind and the lame can’t come into the house.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: David lived in the stronghold, and called it David’s city. David built around from Millo and inward.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: David grew greater and greater; for Yahweh, the God of Armies, was with him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Hiram king of Tyre sent messengers to David, with cedar trees, carpenters, and masons; and they built David a house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: David perceived that Yahweh had established him king over Israel, and that he had exalted his kingdom for his people Israel’s sake.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: David took more concubines and wives for himself out of Jerusalem, after he had come from Hebron; and more sons and daughters were born to David.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: These are the names of those who were born to him in Jerusalem: Shammua, Shobab, Nathan, Solomon,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Ibhar, Elishua, Nepheg, Japhia,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Elishama, Eliada, and Eliphelet.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the Philistines heard that they had anointed David king over Israel, all the Philistines went up to seek David, but David heard about it and went down to the stronghold.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now the Philistines had come and spread themselves in the valley of Rephaim.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: David inquired of Yahweh, saying, “Shall I go up against the Philistines? Will you deliver them into my hand?” Yahweh said to David, “Go up; for I will certainly deliver the Philistines into your hand.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: David came to Baal Perazim, and David struck them there. Then he said, “Yahweh has broken my enemies before me, like the breach of waters.” Therefore he called the name of that place Baal Perazim.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They left their images there; and David and his men took them away.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The Philistines came up yet again, and spread themselves in the valley of Rephaim.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When David inquired of Yahweh, he said, “You shall not go up. Circle around behind them, and attack them in front of the mulberry trees.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When you hear the sound of marching in the tops of the mulberry trees, then stir yourself up; for then Yahweh has gone out before you to strike the army of the Philistines.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: David did so, as Yahweh commanded him, and struck the Philistines all the way from Geba to Gezer.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: David again gathered together all the chosen men of Israel, thirty thousand.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: David arose, and went with all the people who were with him, from Baale Judah, to bring up from there God’s ark, which is called by the Name, even the name of Yahweh of Armies who sits above the cherubim.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They set God’s ark on a new cart, and brought it out of Abinadab’s house that was on the hill; and Uzzah and Ahio, the sons of Abinadab, drove the new cart.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They brought it out of Abinadab’s house, which was in the hill, with God’s ark; and Ahio went before the ark.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: David and all the house of Israel played before Yahweh with all kinds of instruments made of cypress wood, with harps, with stringed instruments, with tambourines, with castanets, and with cymbals.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When they came to the threshing floor of Nacon, Uzzah reached for God’s ark, and took hold of it; for the cattle stumbled.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh’s anger burned against Uzzah; and God struck him there for his error; and he died there by God’s ark.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: David was displeased, because Yahweh had broken out against Uzzah; and he called that place Perez Uzzah,  to this day.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: David was afraid of Yahweh that day; and he said, “How could Yahweh’s ark come to me?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So David would not move Yahweh’s ark to be with him in David’s city; but David carried it aside into Obed-Edom the Gittite’s house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh’s ark remained in Obed-Edom the Gittite’s house three months; and Yahweh blessed Obed-Edom and all his house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: King David was told, “Yahweh has blessed the house of Obed-Edom, and all that belongs to him, because of God’s ark.” So David went and brought up God’s ark from the house of Obed-Edom into David’s city with joy.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When those who bore Yahweh’s ark had gone six paces, he sacrificed an ox and a fattened calf.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: David danced before Yahweh with all his might; and David was clothed in a linen ephod.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So David and all the house of Israel brought up Yahweh’s ark with shouting, and with the sound of the trumpet.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As Yahweh’s ark came into David’s city, Michal the daughter of Saul looked out through the window, and saw king David leaping and dancing before Yahweh; and she despised him in her heart.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They brought in Yahweh’s ark, and set it in its place, in the middle of the tent that David had pitched for it; and David offered burnt offerings and peace offerings before Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When David had finished offering the burnt offering and the peace offerings, he blessed the people in the name of Yahweh of Armies.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He gave to all the people, even among the whole multitude of Israel, both to men and women, to everyone a portion of bread, dates, and raisins. So all the people departed, each to his own house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then David returned to bless his household. Michal the daughter of Saul came out to meet David, and said, “How glorious the king of Israel was today, who uncovered himself today in the eyes of the servants of his servants, as one of the vain fellows shamelessly uncovers himself!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: David said to Michal, “It was before Yahweh, who chose me above your father, and above all his house, to appoint me prince over the people of Yahweh, over Israel. Therefore I will celebrate before Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will be yet more vile than this, and will be base in my own sight. But of the servants of whom you have spoken, they will honor me.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Michal the daughter of Saul had no child to the day of her death.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the king lived in his house, and Yahweh had given him rest from all his enemies all around,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the king said to Nathan the prophet, “See now, I dwell in a house of cedar, but God’s ark dwells within curtains.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Nathan said to the king, “Go, do all that is in your heart; for Yahweh is with you.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: That same night, Yahweh’s word came to Nathan, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Go and tell my servant David, ‘Yahweh says, “Should you build me a house for me to dwell in?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For I have not lived in a house since the day that I brought the children of Israel up out of Egypt, even to this day, but have moved around in a tent and in a tabernacle.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In all places in which I have walked with all the children of Israel, did I say a word to any of the tribes of Israel, whom I commanded to be shepherd of my people Israel, saying, ‘Why have you not built me a house of cedar?’”’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now therefore tell my servant David this, ‘Yahweh of Armies says, “I took you from the sheep pen, from following the sheep, to be prince over my people, over Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I have been with you wherever you went, and have cut off all your enemies from before you. I will make you a great name, like the name of the great ones who are in the earth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will appoint a place for my people Israel, and will plant them, that they may dwell in their own place, and be moved no more. The children of wickedness will not afflict them any more, as at the first,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and as from the day that I commanded judges to be over my people Israel. I will cause you to rest from all your enemies. Moreover Yahweh tells you that Yahweh will make you a house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When your days are fulfilled, and you sleep with your fathers, I will set up your offspring  after you, who will proceed out of your body, and I will establish his kingdom.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He will build a house for my name, and I will establish the throne of his kingdom forever.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will be his father, and he will be my son. If he commits iniquity, I will chasten him with the rod of men, and with the stripes of the children of men;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: but my loving kindness will not depart from him, as I took it from Saul, whom I put away before you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Your house and your kingdom will be made sure forever before you. Your throne will be established forever.”’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Nathan spoke to David all these words, and according to all this vision.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then David the king went in, and sat before Yahweh; and he said, “Who am I, Lord  Yahweh, and what is my house, that you have brought me this far?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: This was yet a small thing in your eyes, Lord Yahweh; but you have spoken also of your servant’s house for a great while to come; and this among men, Lord Yahweh!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: What more can David say to you? For you know your servant, Lord Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For your word’s sake, and according to your own heart, you have worked all this greatness, to make your servant know it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore you are great, Yahweh God. For there is no one like you, neither is there any God besides you, according to all that we have heard with our ears.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: What one nation in the earth is like your people, even like Israel, whom God went to redeem to himself for a people, and to make himself a name, and to do great things for you, and awesome things for your land, before your people, whom you redeemed to yourself out of Egypt, from the nations and their gods?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You established for yourself your people Israel to be your people forever; and you, Yahweh, became their God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now, Yahweh God, the word that you have spoken concerning your servant, and concerning his house, confirm it forever, and do as you have spoken.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let your name be magnified forever, saying, ‘Yahweh of Armies is God over Israel; and the house of your servant David will be established before you.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For you, Yahweh of Armies, the God of Israel, have revealed to your servant, saying, ‘I will build you a house.’ Therefore your servant has found in his heart to pray this prayer to you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Now, O Lord Yahweh, you are God, and your words are truth, and you have promised this good thing to your servant.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now therefore let it please you to bless the house of your servant, that it may continue forever before you; for you, Lord Yahweh, have spoken it. Let the house of your servant be blessed forever with your blessing.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: After this, David struck the Philistines and subdued them; and David took the bridle of the mother city out of the hand of the Philistines.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He struck Moab, and measured them with the line, making them to lie down on the ground; and he measured two lines to put to death, and one full line to keep alive. The Moabites became servants to David, and brought tribute.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: David struck also Hadadezer the son of Rehob, king of Zobah, as he went to recover his dominion at the River.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: David took from him one thousand seven hundred horsemen and twenty thousand footmen. David hamstrung all the chariot horses, but reserved of them for one hundred chariots.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the Syrians of Damascus came to help Hadadezer king of Zobah, David struck twenty two thousand men of the Syrians.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then David put garrisons in Syria of Damascus; and the Syrians became servants to David, and brought tribute. Yahweh gave victory to David wherever he went.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: David took the shields of gold that were on the servants of Hadadezer, and brought them to Jerusalem.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: From Betah and from Berothai, cities of Hadadezer, king David took a great quantity of brass.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Toi king of Hamath heard that David had struck all the army of Hadadezer,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: then Toi sent Joram his son to king David, to greet him, and to bless him, because he had fought against Hadadezer and struck him; for Hadadezer had wars with Toi. Joram brought with him vessels of silver, vessels of gold, and vessels of brass:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: King David also dedicated these to Yahweh, with the silver and gold that he dedicated of all the nations which he subdued;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: of Syria, of Moab, of the children of Ammon, of the Philistines, of Amalek, and of the plunder of Hadadezer, son of Rehob, king of Zobah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: David earned a reputation when he returned from striking down eighteen thousand men of the Syrians in the Valley of Salt.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He put garrisons in Edom. Throughout all Edom put he garrisons, and all the Edomites became servants to David. Yahweh gave victory to David wherever he went.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: David reigned over all Israel; and David executed justice and righteousness for all his people.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Joab the son of Zeruiah was over the army, Jehoshaphat the son of Ahilud was recorder,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Zadok the son of Ahitub and Ahimelech the son of Abiathar were priests, Seraiah was scribe,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Benaiah the son of Jehoiada was over the Cherethites and the Pelethites, David’s sons were chief ministers.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: David said, “Is there yet any who is left of Saul’s house, that I may show him kindness for Jonathan’s sake?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There was of Saul’s house a servant whose name was Ziba, and they called him to David; and the king said to him, “Are you Ziba?” He said, “I am your servant.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The king said, “Is there not yet any of Saul’s house, that I may show the kindness of God to him?” Ziba said to the king, “Jonathan still has a son, who is lame in his feet.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The king said to him, “Where is he?” Ziba said to the king, “Behold, he is in the house of Machir the son of Ammiel, in Lo Debar.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then king David sent, and brought him out of the house of Machir the son of Ammiel, from Lo Debar.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Mephibosheth, the son of Jonathan, the son of Saul, came to David, and fell on his face, and showed respect. David said, “Mephibosheth.” He answered, “Behold, your servant!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: David said to him, “Don’t be afraid of him; for I will surely show you kindness for Jonathan your father’s sake, and will restore to you all the land of Saul your father. You will eat bread at my table continually.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He bowed down, and said, “What is your servant, that you should look at such a dead dog as I am?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then the king called to Ziba, Saul’s servant, and said to him, “All that belonged to Saul and to all his house I have given to your master’s son.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Till the land for him, you, your sons, and your servants. Bring in the harvest, that your master’s son may have bread to eat; but Mephibosheth your master’s son will always eat bread at my table.” Now Ziba had fifteen sons and twenty servants.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Ziba said to the king, “According to all that my lord the king commands his servant, so your servant will do.” So Mephibosheth ate at the king’s table, like one of the king’s sons.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Mephibosheth had a young son, whose name was Mica. All that lived in Ziba’s house were servants to Mephibosheth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So Mephibosheth lived in Jerusalem; for he ate continually at the king’s table. He was lame in both his feet.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: After this, the king of the children of Ammon died, and Hanun his son reigned in his place.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: David said, “I will show kindness to Hanun the son of Nahash, as his father showed kindness to me.” So David sent by his servants to comfort him concerning his father. David’s servants came into the land of the children of Ammon.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But the princes of the children of Ammon said to Hanun their lord, “Do you think that David honors your father, in that he has sent comforters to you? Hasn’t David sent his servants to you to search the city, to spy it out, and to overthrow it?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So Hanun took David’s servants, shaved off one half of their beards, and cut off their garments in the middle, even to their buttocks, and sent them away.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When they told David this, he sent to meet them, for the men were greatly ashamed. The king said, “Wait at Jericho until your beards have grown, and then return.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the children of Ammon saw that they had become odious to David, the children of Ammon sent and hired the Syrians of Beth Rehob, and the Syrians of Zobah, twenty thousand footmen, and the king of Maacah with one thousand men, and the men of Tob twelve thousand men.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When David heard of it, he sent Joab, and all the army of the mighty men.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The children of Ammon came out, and put the battle in array at the entrance of the gate. The Syrians of Zobah and of Rehob, and the men of Tob and Maacah, were by themselves in the field.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now when Joab saw that the battle was set against him before and behind, he chose of all the choice men of Israel, and put them in array against the Syrians.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The rest of the people he committed into the hand of Abishai his brother; and he put them in array against the children of Ammon.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, “If the Syrians are too strong for me, then you shall help me; but if the children of Ammon are too strong for you, then I will come and help you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Be courageous, and let us be strong for our people, and for the cities of our God; and may Yahweh do what seems good to him.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So Joab and the people who were with him came near to the battle against the Syrians, and they fled before him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the children of Ammon saw that the Syrians had fled, they likewise fled before Abishai, and entered into the city. Then Joab returned from the children of Ammon, and came to Jerusalem.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the Syrians saw that they were defeated by Israel, they gathered themselves together.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Hadadezer sent, and brought out the Syrians who were beyond the River: and they came to Helam, with Shobach the captain of the army of Hadadezer at their head.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: David was told that; and he gathered all Israel together, passed over the Jordan, and came to Helam. The Syrians set themselves in array against David, and fought with him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The Syrians fled before Israel; and David killed seven hundred charioteers of the Syrians, and forty thousand horsemen, and struck Shobach the captain of their army, so that he died there.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When all the kings who were servants to Hadadezer saw that they were defeated before Israel, they made peace with Israel, and served them. So the Syrians were afraid to help the children of Ammon any more.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: At the return of the year, at the time when kings go out, David sent Joab, and his servants with him, and all Israel; and they destroyed the children of Ammon, and besieged Rabbah. But David stayed at Jerusalem.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: At evening, David arose from his bed and walked on the roof of the king’s house. From the roof, he saw a woman bathing, and the woman was very beautiful to look at.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: David sent and inquired after the woman. One said, “Isn’t this Bathsheba, the daughter of Eliam, Uriah the Hittite’s wife?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: David sent messengers, and took her; and she came in to him, and he lay with her (for she was purified from her uncleanness); and she returned to her house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The woman conceived; and she sent and told David, and said, “I am with child.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: David sent to Joab, “Send me Uriah the Hittite.” Joab sent Uriah to David.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Uriah had come to him, David asked him how Joab did, and how the people fared, and how the war prospered.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: David said to Uriah, “Go down to your house and wash your feet.” Uriah departed out of the king’s house, and a gift from the king was sent after him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But Uriah slept at the door of the king’s house with all the servants of his lord, and didn’t go down to his house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When they had told David, saying, “Uriah didn’t go down to his house,” David said to Uriah, “Haven’t you come from a journey? Why didn’t you go down to your house?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Uriah said to David, “The ark, Israel, and Judah, are staying in tents; and my lord Joab and the servants of my lord, are encamped in the open field. Shall I then go into my house to eat and to drink, and to lie with my wife? As you live, and as your soul lives, I will not do this thing!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: David said to Uriah, “Stay here today also, and tomorrow I will let you depart.” So Uriah stayed in Jerusalem that day, and the next day.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When David had called him, he ate and drink before him; and he made him drunk. At evening, he went out to lie on his bed with the servants of his lord, but didn’t go down to his house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the morning, David wrote a letter to Joab, and sent it by the hand of Uriah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He wrote in the letter, saying, “Send Uriah to the forefront of the hottest battle, and retreat from him, that he may be struck, and die.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Joab kept watch on the city, he assigned Uriah to the place where he knew that valiant men were.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The men of the city went out, and fought with Joab. Some of the people fell, even of David’s servants; and Uriah the Hittite died also.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Joab sent and told David all the things concerning the war;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and he commanded the messenger, saying, “When you have finished telling all the things concerning the war to the king,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: it shall be that, if the king’s wrath arise, and he asks you, ‘Why did you go so near to the city to fight? Didn’t you know that they would shoot from the wall?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Who struck Abimelech the son of Jerubbesheth? Didn’t a woman cast an upper millstone on him from the wall, so that he died at Thebez? Why did you go so near the wall?’ then you shall say, ‘Your servant Uriah the Hittite is also dead.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So the messenger went, and came and showed David all that Joab had sent him for.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The messenger said to David, “The men prevailed against us, and came out to us into the field, and we were on them even to the entrance of the gate.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The shooters shot at your servants from off the wall; and some of the king’s servants are dead, and your servant Uriah the Hittite is also dead.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then David said to the messenger, “Tell Joab, ‘Don’t let this thing displease you, for the sword devours one as well as another. Make your battle stronger against the city, and overthrow it.’ Encourage him.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Uriah’s wife heard that Uriah her husband was dead, she mourned for her husband.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the mourning was past, David sent and took her home to his house, and she became his wife, and bore him a son. But the thing that David had done displeased Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh sent Nathan to David. He came to him, and said to him, “There were two men in one city; the one rich, and the other poor.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The rich man had very many flocks and herds,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: but the poor man had nothing, except one little ewe lamb, which he had bought and raised. It grew up together with him, and with his children. It ate of his own food, drank of his own cup, and lay in his bosom, and was like a daughter to him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A traveler came to the rich man, and he spared to take of his own flock and of his own herd, to prepare for the wayfaring man who had come to him, but took the poor man’s lamb, and prepared it for the man who had come to him.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: David’s anger burned hot against the man, and he said to Nathan, “As Yahweh lives, the man who has done this deserves to die!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He must restore the lamb fourfold, because he did this thing, and because he had no pity!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Nathan said to David, “You are the man. This is what Yahweh, the God of Israel, says: ‘I anointed you king over Israel, and I delivered you out of the hand of Saul.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I gave you your master’s house, and your master’s wives into your bosom, and gave you the house of Israel and of Judah; and if that would have been too little, I would have added to you many more such things.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Why have you despised Yahweh’s word, to do that which is evil in his sight? You have struck Uriah the Hittite with the sword, and have taken his wife to be your wife, and have slain him with the sword of the children of Ammon.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now therefore the sword will never depart from your house, because you have despised me, and have taken Uriah the Hittite’s wife to be your wife.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “This is what Yahweh says: ‘Behold, I will raise up evil against you out of your own house; and I will take your wives before your eyes, and give them to your neighbor, and he will lie with your wives in the sight of this sun.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For you did this secretly, but I will do this thing before all Israel, and before the sun.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: David said to Nathan, “I have sinned against Yahweh.” Nathan said to David, “Yahweh also has put away your sin. You will not die.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: However, because by this deed you have given great occasion to Yahweh’s enemies to blaspheme, the child also who is born to you will surely die.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Nathan departed to his house. Yahweh struck the child that Uriah’s wife bore to David, and it was very sick.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: David therefore begged God for the child; and David fasted, and went in, and lay all night on the ground.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The elders of his house arose beside him, to raise him up from the earth: but he would not, and he didn’t eat bread with them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: On the seventh day, the child died. David’s servants were afraid to tell him that the child was dead, for they said, “Behold, while the child was yet alive, we spoke to him, and he didn’t listen to our voice. How will he then harm himself, if we tell him that the child is dead?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But when David saw that his servants were whispering together, David perceived that the child was dead; and David said to his servants, “Is the child dead?” They said, “He is dead.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then David arose from the earth, and washed, and anointed himself, and changed his clothing; and he came into Yahweh’s house, and worshiped. Then he came to his own house; and when he requested, they set bread before him, and he ate.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then his servants said to him, “What is this that you have done? You fasted and wept for the child while he was alive, but when the child was dead, you rose up and ate bread.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, “While the child was yet alive, I fasted and wept; for I said, ‘Who knows whether Yahweh will not be gracious to me, that the child may live?’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But now he is dead, why should I fast? Can I bring him back again? I will go to him, but he will not return to me.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: David comforted Bathsheba his wife, and went in to her, and lay with her. She bore a son, and he called his name Solomon. Yahweh loved him;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and he sent by the hand of Nathan the prophet, and he named him Jedidiah,  for Yahweh’s sake.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now Joab fought against Rabbah of the children of Ammon, and took the royal city.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Joab sent messengers to David, and said, “I have fought against Rabbah. Yes, I have taken the city of waters.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now therefore gather the rest of the people together, and encamp against the city, and take it; lest I take the city, and it be called by my name.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: David gathered all the people together, and went to Rabbah, and fought against it, and took it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He took the crown of their king from off his head; and its weight was a talent  of gold, and in it were precious stones; and it was set on David’s head. He brought a great quantity of plunder out of the city.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He brought out the people who were in it, and put them under saws, under iron picks, under axes of iron, and made them pass through the brick kiln; and he did so to all the cities of the children of Ammon. Then David and all the people returned to Jerusalem.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: After this, Absalom the son of David had a beautiful sister, whose name was Tamar; and Amnon the son of David loved her.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Amnon was so troubled that he became sick because of his sister Tamar; for she was a virgin; and it seemed hard to Amnon to do anything to her.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But Amnon had a friend, whose name was Jonadab, the son of Shimeah, David’s brother; and Jonadab was a very subtle man.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to him, “Why, son of the king, are you so sad from day to day? Won’t you tell me?” Amnon said to him, “I love Tamar, my brother Absalom’s sister.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jonadab said to him, “Lay down on your bed, and pretend to be sick. When your father comes to see you, tell him, ‘Please let my sister Tamar come and give me bread to eat, and prepare the food in my sight, that I may see it, and eat it from her hand.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So Amnon lay down and faked being sick. When the king came to see him, Amnon said to the king, “Please let my sister Tamar come, and make me a couple of cakes in my sight, that I may eat from her hand.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then David sent home to Tamar, saying, “Go now to your brother Amnon’s house, and prepare food for him.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So Tamar went to her brother Amnon’s house; and he was lying down. She took dough, and kneaded it, made cakes in his sight, and baked the cakes.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She took the pan, and poured them out before him; but he refused to eat. Amnon said, “Have all men leave me.” Then every man went out from him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Amnon said to Tamar, “Bring the food into the room, that I may eat from your hand.” Tamar took the cakes which she had made, and brought them into the room to Amnon her brother.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When she had brought them near to him to eat, he took hold of her, and said to her, “Come, lie with me, my sister!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She answered him, “No, my brother, do not force me! For no such thing ought to be done in Israel. Don’t you do this folly.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As for me, where would I carry my shame? And as for you, you will be as one of the fools in Israel. Now therefore, please speak to the king; for he will not withhold me from you.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: However he would not listen to her voice; but being stronger than she, he forced her, and lay with her.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Amnon hated her with exceedingly great hatred; for the hatred with which he hated her was greater than the love with which he had loved her. Amnon said to her, “Arise, be gone!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She said to him, “Not so, because this great wrong in sending me away is worse than the other that you did to me!” But he would not listen to her.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then he called his servant who ministered to him, and said, “Now put this woman out from me, and bolt the door after her.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She had a garment of various colors on her; for the king’s daughters who were virgins dressed in such robes. Then his servant brought her out and bolted the door after her.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Tamar put ashes on her head, and tore her garment of various colors that was on her; and she laid her hand on her head, and went her way, crying aloud as she went.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Absalom her brother said to her, “Has Amnon your brother been with you? But now hold your peace, my sister. He is your brother. Don’t take this thing to heart.” So Tamar remained desolate in her brother Absalom’s house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But when king David heard of all these things, he was very angry.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Absalom spoke to Amnon neither good nor bad; for Absalom hated Amnon, because he had forced his sister Tamar.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: After two full years, Absalom had sheep shearers in Baal Hazor, which is beside Ephraim: and Absalom invited all the king’s sons.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Absalom came to the king, and said, “See now, your servant has sheep shearers. Please let the king and his servants go with your servant.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The king said to Absalom, “No, my son, let us not all go, lest we be burdensome to you.” He pressed him; however he would not go, but blessed him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Absalom said, “If not, please let my brother Amnon go with us.” The king said to him, “Why should he go with you?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But Absalom pressed him, and he let Amnon and all the king’s sons go with him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Absalom commanded his servants, saying, “Mark now, when Amnon’s heart is merry with wine; and when I tell you, ‘Strike Amnon,’ then kill him. Don’t be afraid. Haven’t I commanded you? Be courageous, and be valiant!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The servants of Absalom did to Amnon as Absalom had commanded. Then all the king’s sons arose, and every man got up on his mule, and fled.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: While they were on the way, the news came to David, saying, “Absalom has slain all the king’s sons, and there is not one of them left!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then the king arose, and tore his garments, and lay on the earth; and all his servants stood by with their clothes torn.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jonadab, the son of Shimeah, David’s brother, answered, “Don’t let my lord suppose that they have killed all the young men the king’s sons; for Amnon only is dead; for by the appointment of Absalom this has been determined from the day that he forced his sister Tamar.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now therefore don’t let my lord the king take the thing to his heart, to think that all the king’s sons are dead; for only Amnon is dead.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But Absalom fled. The young man who kept the watch lifted up his eyes, and looked, and behold, many people were coming by way of the hillside behind him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jonadab said to the king, “Behold, the king’s sons are coming! It is as your servant said.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As soon as he had finished speaking, behold, the king’s sons came, and lifted up their voice, and wept. The king also and all his servants wept bitterly.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But Absalom fled, and went to Talmai the son of Ammihur, king of Geshur. David mourned for his son every day.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So Absalom fled, and went to Geshur, and was there three years.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: King David longed to go out to Absalom; for he was comforted concerning Amnon, since he was dead.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now Joab the son of Zeruiah perceived that the king’s heart was toward Absalom.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Joab sent to Tekoa, and brought a wise woman from there, and said to her, “Please act like a mourner, and put on mourning clothing, please, and don’t anoint yourself with oil, but be as a woman who has mourned a long time for the dead.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Go in to the king, and speak like this to him.” So Joab put the words in her mouth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the woman of Tekoa spoke to the king, she fell on her face to the ground, showed respect, and said, “Help, O king!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The king said to her, “What ails you?” She answered, “Truly I am a widow, and my husband is dead.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Your servant had two sons, and they both fought together in the field, and there was no one to part them, but the one struck the other, and killed him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, the whole family has risen against your servant, and they say, ‘Deliver him who struck his brother, that we may kill him for the life of his brother whom he killed, and so destroy the heir also.’ Thus they would quench my coal which is left, and would leave to my husband neither name nor remainder on the surface of the earth.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The king said to the woman, “Go to your house, and I will give a command concerning you.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The woman of Tekoa said to the king, “My lord, O king, may the iniquity be on me, and on my father’s house; and may the king and his throne be guiltless.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The king said, “Whoever says anything to you, bring him to me, and he will not bother you any more.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then she said, “Please let the king remember Yahweh your God, that the avenger of blood destroy not any more, lest they destroy my son.” He said, “As Yahweh lives, not one hair of your son shall fall to the earth.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then the woman said, “Please let your servant speak a word to my lord the king.” He said, “Say on.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The woman said, “Why then have you devised such a thing against the people of God? For in speaking this word the king is as one who is guilty, in that the king does not bring home again his banished one.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For we must die, and are like water spilled on the ground, which can’t be gathered up again; neither does God take away life, but devises means, that he who is banished not be an outcast from him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now therefore seeing that I have come to speak this word to my lord the king, it is because the people have made me afraid. Your servant said, ‘I will now speak to the king; it may be that the king will perform the request of his servant.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the king will hear, to deliver his servant out of the hand of the man who would destroy me and my son together out of the inheritance of God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then your servant said, ‘Please let the word of my lord the king bring rest; for as an angel of God, so is my lord the king to discern good and bad. May Yahweh, your God, be with you.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then the king answered the woman, “Please don’t hide anything from me that I ask you.” The woman said, “Let my lord the king now speak.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The king said, “Is the hand of Joab with you in all this?” The woman answered, “As your soul lives, my lord the king, no one can turn to the right hand or to the left from anything that my lord the king has spoken; for your servant Joab urged me, and he put all these words in the mouth of your servant;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: to change the face of the matter has your servant Joab done this thing. My lord is wise, according to the wisdom of an angel of God, to know all things that are in the earth.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The king said to Joab, “Behold now, I have done this thing. Go therefore, and bring the young man Absalom back.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Joab fell to the ground on his face, showed respect, and blessed the king. Joab said, “Today your servant knows that I have found favor in your sight, my lord, king, in that the king has performed the request of his servant.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So Joab arose and went to Geshur, and brought Absalom to Jerusalem.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The king said, “Let him return to his own house, but let him not see my face.” So Absalom returned to his own house, and didn’t see the king’s face.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now in all Israel there was no one to be so much praised as Absalom for his beauty. From the sole of his foot even to the crown of his head there was no defect in him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When he cut the hair of his head (now it was at every year’s end that he cut it; because it was heavy on him, therefore he cut it); he weighed the hair of his head at two hundred shekels,  after the king’s weight.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Three sons were born to Absalom, and one daughter, whose name was Tamar. She was a woman with a beautiful face.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Absalom lived two full years in Jerusalem, and he didn’t see the king’s face.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Absalom sent for Joab, to send him to the king, but he would not come to him. Then he sent again a second time, but he would not come.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore he said to his servants, “Behold, Joab’s field is near mine, and he has barley there. Go and set it on fire.” So Absalom’s servants set the field on fire.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Joab arose, and came to Absalom to his house, and said to him, “Why have your servants set my field on fire?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Absalom answered Joab, “Behold, I sent to you, saying, ‘Come here, that I may send you to the king, to say, “Why have I come from Geshur? It would be better for me to be there still. Now therefore let me see the king’s face, and if there is iniquity in me, let him kill me.”’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So Joab came to the king, and told him; and when he had called for Absalom, he came to the king, and bowed himself on his face to the ground before the king; and the king kissed Absalom.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: After this, Absalom prepared a chariot and horses for himself, and fifty men to run before him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Absalom rose up early, and stood beside the way of the gate. When any man had a suit which should come to the king for judgment, then Absalom called to him, and said, “What city are you from?” He said, “Your servant is of one of the tribes of Israel.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Absalom said to him, “Behold, your matters are good and right; but there is no man deputized by the king to hear you.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Absalom said moreover, “Oh that I were made judge in the land, that every man who has any suit or cause might come to me, and I would do him justice!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It was so, that when any man came near to do him obeisance, he stretched out his hand, and took hold of him, and kissed him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Absalom did this sort of thing to all Israel who came to the king for judgment. So Absalom stole the hearts of the men of Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: At the end of forty years, Absalom said to the king, “Please let me go and pay my vow, which I have vowed to Yahweh, in Hebron.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For your servant vowed a vow while I stayed at Geshur in Syria, saying, ‘If Yahweh shall indeed bring me again to Jerusalem, then I will serve Yahweh.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The king said to him, “Go in peace.” So he arose, and went to Hebron.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But Absalom sent spies throughout all the tribes of Israel, saying, “As soon as you hear the sound of the trumpet, then you shall say, ‘Absalom is king in Hebron!’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Two hundred men went with Absalom out of Jerusalem, who were invited, and went in their simplicity; and they didn’t know anything.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Absalom sent for Ahithophel the Gilonite, David’s counselor, from his city, even from Giloh, while he was offering the sacrifices. The conspiracy was strong; for the people increased continually with Absalom.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A messenger came to David, saying, “The hearts of the men of Israel are after Absalom.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: David said to all his servants who were with him at Jerusalem, “Arise, and let us flee; or else none of us will escape from Absalom. Hurry to depart, lest he overtake us quickly, and bring down evil on us, and strike the city with the edge of the sword.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The king’s servants said to the king, “Behold, your servants are ready to do whatever my lord the king chooses.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The king went out, and all his household after him. The king left ten women, who were concubines, to keep the house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The king went out, and all the people after him; and they stayed in Beth Merhak.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All his servants passed on beside him; and all the Cherethites, and all the Pelethites, and all the Gittites, six hundred men who came after him from Gath, passed on before the king.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then the king said to Ittai the Gittite, “Why do you also go with us? Return, and stay with the king; for you are a foreigner, and also an exile. Return to your own place.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Whereas you came but yesterday, should I today make you go up and down with us, since I go where I may? Return, and take back your brothers. Mercy and truth be with you.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Ittai answered the king, and said, “As Yahweh lives, and as my lord the king lives, surely in what place my lord the king is, whether for death or for life, your servant will be there also.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: David said to Ittai, “Go and pass over.” Ittai the Gittite passed over, and all his men, and all the little ones who were with him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All the country wept with a loud voice, and all the people passed over. The king also himself passed over the brook Kidron, and all the people passed over, toward the way of the wilderness.

translations.name: World English Bible
